sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
NEW PASSWORD
Call taken by: Natasha

sections.instruction: Where can each of the following items be found? Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter A-I next to questions 11-16.
sections.passage_or_context:
Locations
A on washing machine
B in hallway cupboard
C in hot water cupboard
D next to back door
E in bathroom
F on top of television
G in bedroom
H under kitchen sink
I above front door

questions.content:

(12) Garage key
(13) Laundry detergent
(14) Beach towels
(15) Bath towels
(16) Light bulbs
options: A on washing machine

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer.
Choose the correct letter A, B or C.

questions.content: Why did Joanne accept the offer from Gregory Associates?
options: A it covered her travel expenses
B it was from a well-known company
C it was the only offer she received
A she found the work routine repetitive
B the staff were not very helpful
C the work was not related to her studies

questions.content: What did Joanne like best about her internship?
options: A observing how the workplace operates
B being responsible for completing projects
C working closely with the project managers

questions.content: What was the hardest part of the internship?
options: A combining it with her studies
B living on so little money
C working such long hours
A changed her mind about her career
B received a job offer from the company
C decided not to continue her studies

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context: How to apply for an internship

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

questions.content:

(33) Areas of focus: Asia Pacific region; urbanizing (including employment and {{BLANK}}); migration, trade
(34) You will develop skills to: Gather data (both {{BLANK}} and textual data)
(35) You will develop skills to: Carefully {{BLANK}} findings
(36) You will develop skills to: {{BLANK}} on a research project
(37) Brief history of development studies: 1950s – the discipline emerges; {{BLANK}} issues are the major consideration
(38) 1970s – development studies became more {{BLANK}} of the establishment.
(39) Questions were raised about power, environmental sustainability and {{BLANK}} issues.
(40) 1980s – today – national governments were no longer as important. Growing interests in small scale practices such as remittances and {{BLANK}}
